channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DRDO inaugurates CBRN Centre in Delhi for Nuclear and Radiological Preparedness
item.description: NEW DELHI: The Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) has inaugurated a state-of-the-art Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) field training and demonstration centre at B...

item.title: PTI raises concerns over Bushra Bibi’s late-night hospital transfer and lack of transparency
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has expressed strong concern and criticism over reports that Bushra Bibi, wife of former prime minister Imran Khan, was taken from Adiala Jail to a hospital late at ...

item.title: DMK to Observe as Vijay’s TVK Attempts to Form Tamil Nadu Government
item.description: In a recent statement to The Times of India, MK Stalin, chief of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), said his party will adopt a wait-and-watch approach as actor-turned-politician Vijay’s Tamilaga Ve...

item.title: NRC Approves Electoral Committee, Seeks Mediation For Unity In Ijaw National Congress
item.description: The National Representative Council (NRC) of the Ijaw National Congress (INC) on Monday approved a vote of confidence for the Justice Francis Tabai-led National Electoral Committee, which managed the ...

item.title: Questions Arise Over Using Rap Lyrics in Death Penalty Cases After Broadnax Execution
item.description: James G. Broadnax was executed in Texas on April 30, 2026, after nearly two decades on death row. Convicted in 2009 by an almost all-white jury for robbery and double murder, Broadnax's lawyers argued...

item.title: Serengeti Boys announce final 25-man squad for U-17 AFCON in Morocco
item.description: Tanzania's U-17 national football team, known as the Serengeti Boys, have their final 25-man squad ready for the CAF U-17 Africa Cup of Nations finals taking place in Morocco from May 13 to June 2, 20...

item.title: Contractor Curlee Discaya resumes Senate detention following medical procedure
item.description: Pacifico 'Curlee' Discaya II, the embattled contractor, has returned to his detention in the Senate after undergoing surgery on his right shoulder. Senate Secretary Mark Llandro “Dong” Mendoza confirm...

item.title: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton make their public debut as a couple in New York
item.description: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton were spotted enjoying a date night in New York City, marking the first time they publicly held hands. The couple, who have been romantically involved since November a...

item.title: Liberal Party leadership debate highlights internal struggles and declining support
item.description: The article argues that the Liberal Party’s consideration of Tony Abbott and Alexander Downer for the role of federal president reflects deeper structural and reputational issues within the party. Onc...

item.title: ARN chairman faces overwhelming shareholder revolt over executive pay but retains position
item.description: Hamish McLennan, chairman of ARN Media, has faced a significant shareholder backlash after an overwhelming 90 per cent vote against the company’s executive remuneration plan at its annual general meet...

item.title: Guests and Influence at Canberra’s Pre-Budget Galas
item.description: Ahead of the Australian federal budget 2026, Canberra is abuzz with pre-budget events where politicians, lobbyists, and industry figures gather to discuss and influence economic priorities. These gala...

item.title: Rubio and Pope Leo to hold tense Vatican talks amid Trump criticisms
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to meet Pope Leo at the Vatican in what many see as a sensitive and potentially tense discussion. The meeting comes at a time when US President Donald Tru...

item.title: Tension as Akpabio and Oshiomhole dispute new Senate eligibility rules
item.description: Yesterday for Senate, wahala burst as Godswill Akpabio and Adams Oshiomhole start shouting match after dem change the Senate rules. New amendment talk say only lawmakers wey don do at least two consec...

item.title: From artist to contender: Justin Clarke’s path to PFL Africa heavyweight
item.description: South African heavyweight MMA fighter Justin Clarke has taken an unconventional route to the Professional Fighters League (PFL) Africa stage, moving from a background in art to competing at a high lev...

item.title: Historic Drop in US Violent Crime Faces Risk from Federal Funding Cuts
item.description: The United States has seen one of the steepest declines in violent crime in modern history, with homicide rates falling to their lowest point in more than a century. In 2025, major cities recorded a 2...

item.title: Albanese Government Issues Guidelines for AI Data Centres Amid Environmental Concerns
item.description: As Australia sees a rapid expansion of massive data centres at the outskirts of cities, the Albanese government has put forward a set of expectations aimed at mitigating their environmental and social...

item.title: Man shoots two sisters-in-law in Raipur after family dispute
item.description: In Raipur, a tragic incident unfolded on the night of May 5, 2026, when a 33-year-old former bouncer, Jitendra Verma, fatally shot his two sisters-in-law at his in-laws' residence. The dispute reporte...

item.title: Tanzanians Adjust Daily Routines as Fuel Prices Keep Rising
item.description: Fuel price hikes in Tanzania are changing how people dey move around, work, and manage their money. In Dar es Salaam, petrol jumped from Sh3,820 to Sh4,115 per litre, making it hard for many to drive ...

item.title: DJ Denno Dies in Luanda Amid Police and Boda Boda Clashes, Wife Shares Final Moments
item.description: Fans and family of Dennis Omondi, widely known as DJ Denno, are mourning his tragic death in Luanda, Vihiga County. The popular disc jockey reportedly died after being caught in the middle of violent ...

item.title: Ruck depth and half-back drive emerge as key factors in AFL 2026 success
item.description: Eight rounds into the 2026 AFL season, a clear pattern has emerged separating the top sides from the rest: dominance in the ruck and the ability to transition quickly from half-back. Teams performing ...
